name: Off-grid and mini-grid rural electrification
description: Design, feasibility assessment, and operation of decentralized solar/microgrid systems for energy access in underserved rural and post-disaster communities. Spans engineering fundamentals (storage, panel performance, business models) through user demand modeling, impact evaluation, and questions of empowerment, maintenance, and whether technology alone delivers universal energy access.

name: Power grid reliability monitoring and analytics
description: Data-driven measurement and analysis of electricity supply quality, outages, and consumption patterns in developing regions. Includes NLP on outage reports, energy usage analysis, and generating/sharing open data on energy systems to inform planning and accountability.

name: Rural and last-mile network connectivity measurement
description: Empirical measurement and system design for internet/cellular connectivity in rural, low-infrastructure, and crisis-affected regions. Covers cellular data quality studies, 2G/3G rural measurements, store-and-forward kiosk networking, and equitable community internet access.

name: Technology for disaster resilience and risk reduction
description: Engineering and information systems designed to support disaster relief, resilience, and risk reduction. Includes systems architecture for disaster risk reduction, disaster-relief-oriented hardware design, and telecom-enabled content platforms for education and resilience.

name: Digital inclusion for marginalized users and small organizations
description: Studies of how underserved populations and small non-profit or micro-entrepreneur organizations adopt, domesticate, and secure digital tools and connectivity. Emphasizes bridging the digital divide, mobile payment adoption, telecenters fostering inclusion, and information security in unequal contexts.
